sections.heading: Titanium Grades and Alloys for CNC Machining
sections.text: Titanium Grades and Alloys for CNC Machining Grade Properties Applications Grade 1 (CP Titanium) Softest grade, highest ductility, excellent corrosion resistance, good cold-forming characteristics. Architecture, automotive, desalination, medical, marine industries. Grade 2 (CP Titanium) Moderate strength, excellent weldability, good resistance to oxidation and corrosion. Aerospace, chemical processing, marine applications. Grade 3 (CP Titanium) Stronger than Grades 1 and 2, excellent corrosion resistance. Aerospace and industrial applications require moderate strength. Grade 4 (CP Titanium) Higher strength than Grades 2 and 3, good corrosion resistance. Aerospace, chemical processing, medic...

sections.heading: CNC Machined Titanium Parts Examples and Applications
sections.text: CNC Machined Titanium Parts Examples and Applications Aerospace Industry: lightweight, high-strength components such as jet engine blades, landing gear, and structural airframe components for aircraft like the Boeing 787. Medical Devices: biocompatible surgical implants like hip and knee replacements, as well as dental implants and surgical tools. Automotive Sector: such as Formula 1 cars, use CNC machined titanium parts for engine components, exhaust systems, and suspension parts. Marine Applications: corrosion-resistant components like propeller shafts, underwater housings, and fasteners for submarines and ships. Defense Industry: lightweight armor plating, missile components, and militar...

sections.heading: Surface Finishing Services for Titanium Parts
sections.text: Surface Finishing Services for Titanium Parts Polishing: a mechanical finishing process that uses abrasives or chemical agents to create a high-gloss, mirror-like finish, improving corrosion resistance, cleanliness, and visual appeal. Anodizing: creates a protective oxide layer on the titanium surface through an electrochemical process, increasing corrosion resistance, enhancing durability, and allowing for various color options. Bead Blasting: propels small glass beads at high speed to produce a matte or satin finish, effectively removing tool marks and imperfections for a smoother appearance. Powder Coating: involves electrostatically applying a dry powder to the titanium surface and curi...

sections.heading: What is Titanium & What is it Used for – Titanium Properties
sections.text: What is Titanium & What is it Used for – Titanium Properties Titanium is a lustrous transition metal with a silver color, low density, and high strength. It’s a typically ideal material for aerospace, medical , military, chemical processing, and marine industry and extreme heat applications. Titanium grade 5 is the most popular titanium alloy. Titanium has some beneficial properties and characteristics like good ductility, high melting point, and low thermal conductivity, it’s resistant to corrosion from sea water, chlorine and more medias, extremely strong but lightweight, and non-magnetic. Compared to other metals, it’s as strong as steel, the density and strength of titanium are higher t...

sections.heading: FAQs
sections.text: FAQs Can titanium be CNC machined? Yes, titanium can be CNC machined, although it presents several challenges due to its properties. What is the machinability of titanium? The machinability of titanium is generally considered to be difficult because of its high tensile strength and tendency to gall, which can lead to tool wear and poor surface finish. What is the most suitable cutting speed for titanium? The most suitable cutting speed for titanium varies depending on the specific grade and tooling used. For milling titanium alloys like Ti-6Al-4V, a good starting point for cutting speeds is approximately 150 to 300 surface feet per minute (SFM), which translates to about 45 to 90 meters per...
